evidence.text: His initial event was in April 2019 in Germany... A second event occurred in Italy the following July 2019... Since returning to the UK, there have been no further events reported... Follow-up in 6 months, sooner if further episodes occur.

ED triage records and associated nursing documentation were available and have been summarised where relevant. The patient describes seizure activity occurring in flurries over several days with intervals of relative calm thereafter, i.e. a stop–start pattern rather than a steady baseline. This pattern was echoed in the ED notes (e.g. two brief events on 14/09 and three overnight on 27/09, then a quiet week). The patient and family report preserved awareness for some events and post-event fatigue for others; there is no consistent lateralising feature described. No clear precipitants identified; sleep disruption and intercurrent viral symptoms were noted preceding one cluster.

Over the past six weeks he has noticed that brief episodes are tending to bunch together after he wakes, occurring on several mornings each week despite taking his tablets as prescribed. He describes these as short spells of disorientation with a metallic taste and a need to pause for 30–60 seconds, without loss of awareness, sometimes repeating two or three times within the same morning.
